title: CSIS analysis: PLA Navy conducts dual deployments in South China Sea and West Pacific during Balikatan
summary: CSIS-affiliated analysis documents simultaneous PLAN deployments near the Philippines and in the West Pacific, representing a strategic messaging campaign to frame Indo-Pacific defense cooperation as escalatory.
contents:
## PLA Navy Dual Deployment Strategy During Balikatan 2026

### Two-Theater Response
The PLA Navy conducted simultaneous deployments in the South China Sea and Western Pacific in response to Balikatan 2026, according to ISW-AEI analysis associated with CSIS research. The Southern Theater Command deployed a four-ship surface action group east of the Luzon Strait while the carrier Liaoning and escorts operated in the South China Sea.

### Strategic Messaging
The PRC explicitly linked its deployments to the multinational exercises — a departure from the 2025 Balikatan response where the carrier Shandong deployed without official acknowledgment of the exercise connection. This evolution signals Beijing's growing willingness to frame regional defense cooperation as a direct provocation warranting military response.

### Coalition-Breaking Goals
Analysts assess the deployments serve PRC coalition-breaking objectives by demonstrating China's ability to surge military presence whenever the Philippines cooperates with the United States and Japan. The PRC views the US-Japan-Philippines defense triangle as a significant threat to its freedom of maneuver in the South China Sea and through the first island chain.

### Capability Demonstration
The commitment of premier naval assets — including the Type 055 destroyer and carrier Liaoning — demonstrates the PLAN's growing blue-water capability but, as ISW notes, peacetime transits through contested waterways do not necessarily indicate wartime operational capability against prepared anti-access/area-denial systems.

**Source:** ISW-AEI China & Taiwan Update
